title: kuhnia eupatorioides var. chlorolepis
intro: Kuhnia eupatorioides var. chlorolepis, also known as American bugleweed or American water-hemlock, is a plant species within the family Asteraceae, native to eastern North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The health benefits of Kuhnia eupatorioides var. chlorolepis include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as relieving inflammation and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also sometimes used in traditional remedies to treat fever and digestive issues.
